currentUse: Active Roman Catholic parish church (Archdiocese of Cincinnati), worshiping as part of the five-church St. Gabriel the Archangel Family of Parishes; Sunday Mass at 10:30am
summary: Holy Cross (Šv. Kryžiaus) at 1924 Leo St in Old North Dayton is standing and remains an active Catholic parish church — it was never closed or sold. It has been listed on the National Register of Historic Places since November 5, 1991 (ref. #91001582), recognized for its 1960s "modern Lithuanian style" redesign by architect Jonas Mulokas honoring churches shuttered by the Soviet regime in Lithuania. Under the Archdiocese of Cincinnati's Beacons of Light restructuring, Holy Cross was grouped effective July 1, 2022 into the St. Gabriel the Archangel Family of Parishes alongside Our Lady of the Rosary (Dayton), St. Christopher (Vandalia), St. John the Baptist (Tipp City), and St. Peter (Huber Heights), led by pastor Fr. Kyle E. Schnippel with three parochial vicars; the parish office is housed at St. Peter in Huber Heights. Holy Cross's own listed schedule is a single Sunday 10:30am Mass. Restructuring pressure inside the family is real: the family's website currently announces that sister church St. Christopher in Vandalia "Closed in August 2026," though no closure of Holy Cross itself is announced. As of a 2021 archdiocesan profile, Holy Cross was the only Lithuanian parish in southern Ohio, still proclaimed two readings in Lithuanian at every Mass, and runs its signature turtle soup and kugelis fundraiser each November; Global True Lithuania describes it as a de facto Lithuanian community hub hosting Kūčios and other gatherings.

developments.headline: Archdiocesan profile affirms living Lithuanian identity
developments.detail: The Catholic Telegraph's 'Did You Know?' feature described Holy Cross as the only Lithuanian parish in southern Ohio, noting two readings still proclaimed in Lithuanian at every Mass, the Mulokas 1963-64 Lithuanian redesign, and the 1965 Shrine of the Three Crosses honoring martyrs under Soviet occupation.

sources.title: Did you know? Holy Cross Dayton

developments.headline: Grouped into St. Gabriel Family of Parishes under Beacons of Light
developments.detail: The Archdiocese of Cincinnati's Beacons of Light pastoral planning placed Holy Cross in Family NE-10, the St. Gabriel the Archangel Family of Parishes, with Our Lady of the Rosary (Dayton), St. Christopher (Vandalia), St. John the Baptist (Tipp City), and St. Peter (Huber Heights), under pastor Fr. Kyle E. Schnippel and three parochial vicars. The archdiocese's family page stated no parishes or schools were being closed at that time.

developments.headline: Sister church St. Christopher (Vandalia) closed August 2026; Holy Cross unaffected so far
developments.detail: The St. Gabriel Family of Parishes homepage announces 'St. Christopher Church Closed in August 2026' — the first church closure within Holy Cross's parish family, signaling active consolidation pressure in the family. No closure or merger of Holy Cross itself is announced; its Sunday 10:30am Mass remains on the family schedule.

conflictsWithArchiveRecord: No contradictions. The archive record (standing, survived inside the diocese, National Register of Historic Places) is confirmed: the building stands, the parish is active, and the NRHP listing is verified (November 5, 1991, ref. #91001582). Two refinements: (1) since July 1, 2022 Holy Cross no longer operates as a fully standalone parish in practice — it is administered within the five-church St. Gabriel the Archangel Family of Parishes under the Archdiocese of Cincinnati's Beacons of Light restructuring, with its office relocated to St. Peter in Huber Heights and one Sunday Mass on site; (2) the archive's 'debt-free' claim could not be independently verified from current sources. The archive note of survival 'via demonstrated vitality and congregational diversification' is consistent with the 2021 archdiocesan profile and the family's current schedule, which includes Spanish-language Masses at other family churches.
gaps: Could not verify whether a weekly Lithuanian-language Mass (claimed in older tertiary sources) or the two Lithuanian readings per Mass (Catholic Telegraph, 2021) continue in 2026 — the current family Mass schedule page lists no Lithuanian-language Mass and the parish page does not mention one; parish bulletins would need to be checked. Could not verify current parishioner count (the ~250 figure circulating online is undated), current finances (archive's 'debt-free'), or whether the November turtle soup/kugelis sale ran in 2024-2025. No press coverage found of Holy Cross-specific preservation campaigns or Beacons of Light decisions naming Holy Cross; the only documented closure in its parish family is St. Christopher (Vandalia), announced on sgfp.org as closed in August 2026 — the fuller story behind that closure (decree, reasons, appeals) was not found. Searches used both 'Holy Cross Lithuanian' and 'Šv. Kryžiaus' Dayton forms; the parish identity match is unambiguous (only Lithuanian parish in southern Ohio, Leo St address consistent across all sources).
